tags.name: USNS General Hoyt S. Vandenberg
tags.qa_notes: Distance from Key West: sources conflict between 6 miles (Wikipedia) and 7 miles (multiple dive operators). Both figures appear in legitimate sources; 7 miles is the more common operator-cited figure. Mercury mission tracking added (was listed as Apollo/Gemini only). Marine life: removed 'bull shark' as regularly resident species — trip reports confirm goliath grouper, tarpon, barracuda as primary species. Hurricane Irma 2017 shift documented. Scuba Diving Magazine #1 reader-poll ranking confirmed.

tags.name: Spiegel Grove
tags.qa_notes: Displacement conflict: Wikipedia states 11,525 long tons (full load); secondary dive sources round to ~9,000 tons. Likely reflects different load configurations. Premature sinking: confirmed ~6 hours ahead of schedule. Hurricane Dennis 2005 confirmed upright orientation. 144 fish species figure confirmed from florida-scubadiving.com. Spiegel Grove named for Rutherford B. Hayes estate in Fremont, Ohio confirmed.

tags.name: Triumph Reef
tags.note: Lesser-known Key Largo/Biscayne area reef site. No site-specific dive operator listings or trip reports found in three independent searches. Not listed on any dive operator site pages reviewed.
tags.qa_notes: QA re-searched 2026-05-27. No public dive source found for this specific reef. Cannot validate. validated reset to false.

tags.description: Molasses Reef is a world-renowned, easily accessible coral reef located southeast of Key Largo in the Florida Keys National Marine Sanctuary, famous for its clear water, spur-and-groove topography, and diverse marine life across 30+ named mooring buoy sub-sites.
tags.name: Molasses Reef
tags.qa_notes: Eagle Ray Alley: removed 'reliable ray sightings' claim. ScubaBoard confirms eagle rays are not guaranteed and no more likely here than elsewhere on Molasses reef. Yellowhead jawfish and stingrays are the reliable sand-channel species. Depth range re-confirmed as 6ft shallow to 90ft+ outer ledges. 2023 bleaching event documented by NOAA.

tags.name: USCGC Duane (Wreck)
tags.qa_notes: U-boat identity confirmed as U-175 via Wikipedia, uboat.net, USNI, US Coast Guard sources. NOAA Shipwreck Trail page erroneously states 'U-77' — this is documented as a page error; all authoritative sources confirm U-175. Duane recovered 22 survivors confirmed. Keel/launch/commission dates confirmed. NRHP listing date confirmed.

tags.name: Hen and Chickens reef
tags.qa_notes: Site confirmed as Sanctuary Preservation Area with brick barge wreck. ESA-listed star and brain corals confirmed. Brick barge date (1949) verified via NOAA FKNMS wreck page. Site-specific marine life (tarpon, barracuda at barge; eagle rays and yellowtail across patch reef) confirmed from multiple sources.

tags.name: USS Alligator Shipwreck
tags.qa_notes: Wreck identity uncertainty confirmed by Wikipedia and NOAA: 1996 expedition proved the ballast pile at the marked location is not definitively the USS Alligator. Our markdown correctly notes this uncertainty. Marine life: 'over 500 species' removed from markdown — this was a generic SPA figure, not site-specific. Replaced with note that no site-specific marine life sources were located.

tags.name: The Lugano
tags.qa_notes: Vessel history confirmed via NPS Biscayne: British iron-hulled cargo steamer, 350ft, 3,770 tons, built 1882, grounded March 9 1913 on Long Reef. NPS coordinates confirmed. Part of Maritime Heritage Trail confirmed. Cargo details (silks, wines, rice, 116 passengers, $1 million value) confirmed from NPS source.

tags.name: The Mandalay
tags.qa_notes: Vessel history confirmed via NPS Biscayne: 110ft 6in steel auxiliary schooner, built 1928 by George Lawley & Son, grounded January 1 1966 on Long Reef. Captain identified as Asmund (Jim) Gjevick, 26-year-old Norwegian. Original name 'Hardi Biou' then 'Valor' before Windjammer renamed her Mandalay in 1965. 23 passengers + 12 crew rescued by Coast Guard helicopters. Markdown updated to add NPS-confirmed captain details.

tags.name: USS Amesbury(stern)
tags.qa_notes: CORRECTED: LST-496 rescue date was wrong in previous markdown (said June 6 / D-Day; correct date is June 11, 1944, confirmed by Naval History and Heritage Command and Wikipedia). CORRECTED: launch date was June 6 in prior description; correct launch date is June 5, 1943 (Wikipedia). Displacement: standard 1,400 tons / full load 1,740 tons (Buckley class standard). Sold to Chet Alexander Marine Salvage confirmed.

tags.qa_notes: QA re-searched 2026-05-27. No public dive source found. Cannot validate. validated reset to false.

tags.name: Wisteria
tags.note: Wreck tagged in OSM west of Key West. No vessel name, history, dimensions, or dive-specific records found in multiple independent searches 2026-05-27. Identity and condition unknown.
tags.qa_notes: QA re-searched 2026-05-27. No public records found for a dive site or wreck called 'Wisteria' near this location. Cannot validate identity or history. validated reset to false.

tags.name: USS Amesbury (bow)
tags.qa_notes: CORRECTED: LST-496 rescue date corrected from June 6 to June 11, 1944. CORRECTED: launch date corrected from June 6 to June 5, 1943. Vessel history cross-checked across Wikipedia, NOAA, and NHCC. All other historical facts confirmed.
